requirements.title: CRC Streaming Update
requirements.description: Each CRC function shall accept a running CRC value, data buffer, and size, returning the updated CRC value.
requirements.rationale: Streaming CRC computation allows data integrity checks over arbitrarily large or fragmented data without requiring the entire payload to be buffered in memory.

requirements.title: CRC Null Safety
requirements.description: Each CRC function shall return 0 when passed a NULL data pointer.
requirements.rationale: Returning a defined value for NULL input ensures the function is safe to call unconditionally, aligning with the no-silent-failures constraint by producing a predictable result rather than crashing.

requirements.title: CRC Zero Length Safety
requirements.description: Each CRC function shall return 0 when passed a zero data size.
requirements.rationale: Returning a defined value for zero-length input ensures the function is safe to call on empty buffers without special-casing at the call site.

requirements.title: CRC-16 ARC
requirements.description: The CRC module shall compute a CRC-16/ARC update with initial seed 0.
requirements.rationale: CRC-16/ARC is a widely used 16-bit CRC variant in legacy serial protocols and embedded communication stacks.

requirements.title: CRC-16 BinHex
requirements.description: The CRC module shall compute a BinHex 16-bit CRC update with initial seed 0.
requirements.rationale: BinHex CRC is used in Macintosh file encoding and certain archive formats that embedded systems may need to interoperate with.

requirements.title: CRC-16 CCITT
requirements.description: The CRC module shall compute a CRC-16/CCITT update with initial seed 0xFFFF.
requirements.rationale: CRC-16/CCITT is a standard integrity check in telecommunications (X.25, HDLC) and is commonly required for embedded communication interfaces.

requirements.title: CRC-16 Kermit
requirements.description: The CRC module shall compute a CRC-16/Kermit update with initial seed 0.
requirements.rationale: CRC-16/Kermit is used in the Kermit file transfer protocol and certain embedded serial communication stacks.

requirements.title: CRC-32 CCITT32
requirements.description: The CRC module shall compute a 32-bit CCITT CRC update with initial seed 0xFFFFFFFF.
requirements.rationale: A 32-bit CCITT CRC provides stronger error detection than 16-bit variants and is needed for larger data payloads and storage integrity checks.

requirements.title: CRC-32 ZIP
requirements.description: The CRC module shall compute a CRC-32/ZIP update with initial seed 0xFFFFFFFF.
requirements.rationale: CRC-32/ZIP is the de facto standard for file and stream integrity (ZIP, PNG, Ethernet FCS) and is commonly required for interoperability.

requirements.title: CRC-32 ZIP Final XOR
requirements.description: The CRC-32/ZIP function shall apply a final XOR of 0xFFFFFFFF to the computed value when data is processed.
requirements.rationale: The final XOR is part of the CRC-32/ZIP specification and is required for the computed value to match reference implementations and standard test vectors.
